name: Pickled Beet & Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the beet tops, leaving about 2 inches of stem at each end. Do not peel the beets.
2. Slice the onions into 1/4-inch slices.
3. Bring a pot of water to a boil and add the trimmed beets. Cook for about 5 minutes, or until tender when pierced with a fork.
4. Drain the beets and rinse under cold water to cool them quickly. Slip off the skins and trim any remaining root ends.
5. Slice the cooled beets into 1/4-inch pieces.
6. In a Dutch oven that holds at least 6 quarts, bring the vinegar, sugar, pickling spices, and salt to a boil.
7. Reduce the heat to maintain a simmer and add the sliced onions. Cook for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Remove from heat and stir in the remaining beets.
9. Pack hot beet and onion mixture into sterilized jars, leaving about 1/2-inch headspace.
10. Bring water to a boil in a large pot or canning kettle. Carefully remove each jar from the hot water using tongs, cover with boiling water, and seal tightly.
11. Process filled jars in a boiling water bath for 30 minutes, ensuring that there is at least 1 inch of water covering the jars throughout the process.
12. Store processed jars in a cool place once they have cooled completely.
